tier_label: Perennial All-NBA First Team — elite franchise player

narrative.identity: Stephen Curry is the realized ceiling of the perimeter shot creator. A 4-time NBA champion, 2-time Most Valuable Player, 2022 Finals MVP, 12-time All-Star, and the NBA all-time leader in career 3-pointers made. The question is no longer ascension; it is how much longer the late-career window holds. At 6'2"/185lbs with a 6'4" wingspan, Curry is built slim for the position, with a meticulous training and recovery routine that has carried him through 17 NBA seasons at elite per-game efficiency.
narrative.strength: Offensively, Curry is the historical reference point at his position. Catch-and-shoot 41.0% on 603 attempts since 2024-25, pull-up 3-point 38.0% on 660, and a career free-throw percentage of 91.2% all read as elite volume-plus-conversion. The off-ball gravity is the dimension no peer has matched: cut 1.476 points per possession in 2024-25 at the 82nd percentile, off-screen 1.219 at the 88th, off-ball speed 4.82 mph, and universal recognition as the best movement shooter of all time. The signature handle extends the creation tree to isolation, pick-and-roll, and dribble-handoff weaponry. At age 38 in 2025-26 he dropped 26.6 points per game in 30.9 minutes, the third-most ever for a player 37 or older.
narrative.weakness: Defensively, Curry is the structural weakness in the profile. He is a 6'2", 185-pound point guard with a 6'4" wingspan, a career-long matchup target whom opposing teams isolate every playoff series. Opponents shot 3.0% above their expected field-goal percentage against him in 2025-26, a regression from slightly below expected in 2024-25; help defense reads better than on-ball at 1.1 steals and 2.4 deflections per game, but the team defensive rating of 114.8 reflects mediocre team-level support. The athleticism floor of balanced burst, middling lateral quickness, and slim-frame strength caps the defensive ceiling, compounded at age 38 by the patellofemoral pain syndrome that cost him over two months in 2025-26. He survives via team scheme and competitive effort, not lockdown ability.
narrative.projection_verdict: At 38, Curry has already authored the verdict on his career: four championships, two MVPs including the only unanimous selection in NBA history, a 2022 Finals MVP earned without a co-superstar, and the NBA all-time record for career 3-pointers made, the first player past 4,000. The credentials still arrive: 2023-24 Clutch Player of the Year leading the NBA in total clutch points, 2024 Olympic gold, his 12th All-Star nod in February 2026. On full availability, the ceiling is a vintage swan-song with another deep playoff push and an All-NBA selection within reach; the floor is a sharper late-career decline. The question is whether he closes with one more championship or a quiet bow.

domains.name: Finishing
domains.one_line: Solid rim conversion at 66.2% on 284 attempts since 2024-25 anchors the score, a modest free-throw rate constrains the contact-finishing dimension.

domains.name: Shooting
domains.one_line: NBA all-time leader at 91.2% career on free throws, catch-and-shoot 41.0% on 603 attempts and pull-up 3-point 38.0% on 660 since 2024-25, the historical guard-shooting ceiling.

domains.name: Ball Skills
domains.one_line: Elite touch and signature handle anchor an All-Star creator package, with an assist-to-turnover ratio of 1.85 keeping security below top tier.

domains.name: Playmaking
domains.one_line: Iconic off-ball movement at 4.82 mph speed in 2024-25 drives the domain, secondary-creator vision and decision-making above baseline rather than at All-Star level.

domains.name: Defense
domains.one_line: Career-long perimeter matchup target, opponents shooting 3.0% above expected in 2025-26, partially offset by 1.1 steals and 2.4 deflections per game.

domains.name: Rebounding
domains.one_line: Defensive rebounds 3.9 per game in 2024-25 healthy at the guard position, securing boards when positioned anchors the functional band.

domains.name: Athleticism
domains.one_line: Balanced burst rather than explosive, lateral quickness and 185-pound strength both liabilities at age 38 after the patellofemoral pain syndrome.

domains.name: IQ / Motor
domains.one_line: Iconic competitive character with four rings and two MVPs paired with elite motor at age 38 anchors All-Star IQ above functional shot selection.

sub_domains.name: At-basket finishing
sub_domains.rationale_public: Rim conversion 66.2% on 284 attempts since 2024-25; crafty rather than explosive, with 15.3% of 2025-26 shots at the rim and 61.5% assisted, reducing self-creation.

sub_domains.name: Contact finishing / foul draw
sub_domains.rationale_public: Free-throw rate of 27.7% in 2025-26 modest for a 30.8% usage primary creator; slim-frame structural cap, not a foul-draw archetype despite scoring volume.

sub_domains.name: Post offense
sub_domains.rationale_public: No documented post role; structural absence for the archetype, with slim-frame strength reinforcing the floor.

sub_domains.name: Catch-and-shoot 3PT
sub_domains.rationale_public: Catch-and-shoot 41.0% on 603 attempts since 2024-25; the historical reference point at the position with a career 3-point percentage of 42.2%.

signature.name: Sniper
signature.description: Spot-up shooting off the catch from three. Footwork, release consistency, and range depth on attempts that arrive in rhythm.

sub_domains.name: Off-dribble shooting
sub_domains.rationale_public: Pull-up 3-point 38.0% on 660 attempts since 2024-25; isolation 1.088 points per possession in 2025-26 on 91 possessions (88.7th percentile); the signature step-back redefined the modern pull-up three.

signature.name: Pull-Up Shooter
signature.description: Creating own shot off the dribble. Pull-ups, step-backs, and self-generated separation moves with a clean release over a contest.

sub_domains.name: Mid-range
sub_domains.rationale_public: Mid-range 50.4% on 226 attempts since 2024-25, excellent per-attempt conversion for the guard position.

signature.name: Mid-Range Threat
signature.description: Pull-up and catch-and-shoot scoring from the elbow and baseline mid-range zones. A diminished but real weapon at the NBA level.

sub_domains.name: Free throw
sub_domains.rationale_public: Career free-throw percentage of 91.2%, the NBA all-time leader; 92.9% on 520 attempts since 2024-25, 94.9% in clutch during 2024-25.

sub_domains.name: Handling / creation
sub_domains.rationale_public: Signature dribble package: isolation 1.088 points per possession in 2025-26 on 91 possessions, 88.7th percentile; 7.8 drives per game with 52.0% conversion. The handle creates the shot.

signature.name: Shot Creator
signature.description: Dribble tightness, move variety, and live-dribble creation. Beats defenders with handle alone and generates scoring opportunities for self or teammates.

sub_domains.name: Touch / feel
sub_domains.rationale_public: All-time leader in career free-throw percentage; floater 48.5% on 361 attempts since 2024-25, non-dunk rim 66.3% on 282; convergent touch signal across all proxies.

signature.name: Soft Touch
signature.description: Fingertip control at the point of contact between hands and ball. Backboard finishes, fingerrolls, soft catches, and accurate pass delivery.

sub_domains.name: Ball security
sub_domains.rationale_public: Assist-to-turnover ratio of 1.85 sits below the 2:1 standard for primary creators on 30.8% usage and 65.4 touches per game.

sub_domains.name: Court vision
sub_domains.rationale_public: Potential assists 10.3 per game in 2024-25 healthy (8.7 in 2025-26), assist points created 15.0; vision better than reputation but operates as secondary creator next to Draymond Green.

sub_domains.name: Decision-making
sub_domains.rationale_public: Clutch Player of the Year 2023-24, leading the NBA in total clutch points; routine-possession lapses pull the average.

sub_domains.name: Passing execution
sub_domains.rationale_public: 42.9 passes per game in 2024-25 healthy (38.9 in 2025-26); delivery solid and on-target in most situations, occasional off-target under pressure. Not a threading-the-needle pure passer.

sub_domains.name: Off-ball movement
sub_domains.rationale_public: Off-ball speed 4.82 mph in 2024-25; cut 1.476 points per possession (82.2nd percentile), off-screen 1.219 (87.7th); universally the best movement shooter of all time.

signature.name: Off-Ball Threat
signature.description: Reading and using screens, cutting decisively, and creating space without the ball. Combines IQ with sustained motion in the half-court.

sub_domains.name: On-ball pressure
sub_domains.rationale_public: Opponents shoot 3.0% above their expected field-goal percentage in 2025-26 (slightly below expected in 2024-25); career-long matchup-hunting target on switches and isolation.

sub_domains.name: Help defense
sub_domains.rationale_public: Steals 1.1 per game and deflections 2.4 in 2025-26; active hands and positive help-IQ reads, with team defensive rating 114.8 capping the team-level signal.

sub_domains.name: Rim protection
sub_domains.rationale_public: Structural absence at 6'2"; 0.4 blocks per game, not part of the defensive identity.

sub_domains.name: Post defense
sub_domains.rationale_public: Slim 185-pound frame creates a structural ceiling; opponents 1.273 points per possession in post-ups in 2025-26; career-long target on switches against physical wing scorers.

sub_domains.name: Offensive rebounding
sub_domains.rationale_public: Offensive rebound rate well below guard average; archetype-appropriate floor-spacer role with no offensive-glass crash.

sub_domains.name: Defensive rebounding
sub_domains.rationale_public: Defensive rebounds 3.9 per game in 2024-25 healthy (3.2 in 2025-26), above-average for the guard position; secures boards when positioned despite the slim frame.

sub_domains.name: Burst / explosion
sub_domains.rationale_public: Balanced rather than explosive; 35.5" max vertical at the 2009 combine, career craft-over-burst profile recalibrated by patellofemoral pain syndrome at age 38.

sub_domains.name: Lateral quickness
sub_domains.rationale_public: Lane agility 11.07 seconds at the 2009 combine; slim frame and the recent knee inflection compound a career-long lateral target.

sub_domains.name: Strength
sub_domains.rationale_public: 185-pound slim frame; physical wings expose the matchup in switch and post coverage despite documented strength-training discipline.

sub_domains.name: Shot selection
sub_domains.rationale_public: Heavy creation burden with 30.8% usage in 2025-26 and 65.4 touches per game; conversion holds at 49.0% on very-tight contests since 2024-25, with discipline below elite-creator tier.

sub_domains.name: Effort / motor
sub_domains.rationale_public: Off-ball speed 4.82 mph in 2025-26 at age 38 carrying 26.6 points per game on a full-volume off-ball workload; durable motor signal across 17 NBA seasons.

signature.name: Hustle
signature.description: Relentless pursuit. Loose balls, transition sprints, deflections, and the second-effort plays that don't appear in the box score but change games.

sub_domains.name: Competitive character
sub_domains.rationale_public: Four-time NBA champion, two-time Most Valuable Player, 2022 Finals MVP, 2023-24 Clutch Player of the Year, 12-time All-Star, 2024 Olympic gold; top-5 active competitive character.

signature.name: Competitor
signature.description: Physicality and intensity when the game is hard. Performance holds in close games, rivalry matchups, and elimination scenarios. Withstands adversity without withdrawing.

projection.spread_note_public: The projection settles at 9.20 with a floor of 8.20 and a swan-song ceiling of 9.25; the late-career window is narrowed by the patellofemoral pain syndrome and slim-frame age decline. Confidence: Moderate.

comps.similarity_public: Reggie Miller is the lineage prototype: shooting gravity, off-ball maestry, and late-career guard longevity. Curry realizes the ceiling Miller foreshadowed, adding handle creation, four rings, two MVPs, and the deep range that redefined the modern offensive era.
